ribbon.title: Openings
ribbon.description: The Computational Multiphysics Research Group (CoMPhys Lab) focuses on developing advanced computational models to study complex thermomechanical systems involving moving boundaries, multiphase interactions, and high strain-rate physics. Our research spans a broad range of applications—from bioinspired flight and cardiovascular flows to droplet evaporation, energetic material dynamics, and hypersonic reentry physics.

sections.title: Applications Open: Research Programmes {Ph.D / M.S. (by Research)} – Jan 2026 Intake @ IIT Madras
sections.description: The Computational Multiphysics Research Group at IIT Madras is inviting motivated candidates to pursue cutting-edge research across the following thrust areas:
points: 🌿 Bioinspired Flows: Unravel unsteady aerodynamics in insect flight and blood flow in cardiovascular systems.
💥 Energetic Material Dynamics: Simulate detonation physics, shock initiation, and reactive flow in energetic environments.
☄️ Hypersonic Reentry Physics: Investigate aerothermal loads, ablation, and gas–surface interactions during extreme reentry conditions.
🛠️ High Strain-Rate Material Response: Explore deformation, failure, and fracture under intense thermo-mechanical loading.
🤖 AI-Driven Multi-Scale Modeling*: Develop next-generation solvers by integrating machine learning with multiscale physics.
info: ✅ Eligibility: Strong academic background in Aerospace, Mechanical, Applied Physics, or allied fields. Admission through written test & interview. Syllabus: https://ae.iitm.ac.in/documents/Interview_Syllabus.pdf
✉️ Contact: Interested candidates may write directly to pkseshadri@iitm.ac.in

sections.title: Undergraduate Research Internships & Projects
points: 🛠️ Gain hands-on experience with computational tools and methods used in scientific research
🧩 Learn to approach problems systematically and strengthen logical reasoning
📖 Develop research discipline through literature study, careful documentation, and critical analysis
info: 📧 Apply: Send your resume and a short statement of motivation (1 paragraph) to pkseshadri@iitm.ac.in

sections.title: High School Research Exposure
points: ☀️ 1-week summer bootcamps led by lab members
💻 Introduction to computational thinking and simple scientific simulations
🧩 Learn how physics, math, and computing come together to model real-world phenomena
🤔 Develop curiosity, logical reasoning, and confidence in exploring scientific questions

sections.title: Application Process
points: 📎 Attach your CV, a short motivation note, and indicate your research area(s) of interest
